channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Foreign ministers meet in Italy for the last G7 of the Biden administration
item.description: FIUGGI, Italy (AP) — Foreign ministers from the world’s leading industrialized nations are meeting Monday, with the wars in Ukraine and the Middle East entering decisive phases and a certain pressure to advance diplomatic efforts ahead of the new U.S. administration taking over.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: South Korea holds memorial for forced laborers at Sado mines, a day after boycotting Japanese event
item.description: SADO, Japan (AP) — South Korea paid tribute to wartime Korean forced laborers at Japan’s Sado Island Gold Mines in a memorial ceremony on Monday, a day after boycotting a similar event organized by Japan, as tensions over historical atrocities continue to strain relations between the two sides.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Landslide and flash floods hit Indonesia’s Sumatra island, leaving 16 dead and 6 missing
item.description: KARO, Indonesia (AP) — Rescuers in Indonesia recovered 16 bodies under tons of mud and rocks or that were swept away in flash floods that hit mountainside villages on Sumatra Island, officials said Monday. Six people are still missing, officials said.
item.dc:creator: BINSAR BAKKARA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DHL cargo plane crashes and skids into a house in Lithuania, killing at least 1
item.description: A DHL cargo plane has crashed on approach to an airport in Lithuania's capital and skidded into a nearby house, leaving at least one person dead.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Lebanon's Shiite Muslims pay high price in war between Israel and Hezbollah
item.description: The Lebanese civilians most devastated by the Israel-Hezbollah war are Shiite Muslims, and many of them believe they are being unfairly punished because they share a religious identity with Hezbollah militants and often live in the same areas.
item.dc:creator: Bassem Mroue And Kareem Chehayeb, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Uruguay’s leftist opposition candidate Yamandú Orsi becomes country's new president
item.description: Uruguay’s leftist opposition candidate, Yamandú Orsi, became the country's new president in a tight runoff election that ousted the conservative governing coalition.
item.dc:creator: Nayara Batschke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New Zealanders save more than 30 stranded whales by lifting them on sheets
item.description: More than 30 pilot whales that stranded themselves on a beach in New Zealand have been safely returned to the ocean after conservation workers and residents helped to refloat them by lifting them on sheets.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel moves toward ceasefire deal with Hezbollah: reports
item.description: Israel is reportedly moving toward a ceasefire agreement with Hezbollah in Lebanon after nearly a year of fighting escalated into an all-out war
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate Intelligence Committee will ask Gabbard 'lots of questions,' Lankford says
item.description: Sen. James Lankford (R-Okla.) said Sunday that the Senate Intelligence Committee will ask President-elect Trump’s pick for director of national intelligence, former Democratic Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), “lots of questions.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Heavy rains in Bolivia send mud crashing into the capital, leaving 1 missing and destroying homes
item.description: LA PAZ, Bolivia (AP) — A landslide caused by heavy rains after a prolonged drought in La Paz, the capital of Bolivia, flooded dozens of homes early Sunday, local authorities said. It swept away a young girl, spurred evacuations and left parts of the city without electricity.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shootout near Israeli Embassy in Jordan leaves 3 police officers injured, gunman dead
item.description: Police in Jordan shot and killed a gunman who wounded three police officers near the Israeli Embassy on Sunday morning, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Haiti wonders what's next as gang violence surges and the push for a UN peacekeeping mission flops
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — When Kenyan police arrived in Haiti as part of a U.N.-backed mission earlier this year to tackle gang violence, hopes were high.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taiwan's former president says US should prioritize helping Ukraine over her country
item.description: Former Taiwanese President Tsai Ing-wen suggested that the U.S. should focus on helping Ukraine before it prioritizes Taiwan in her keynote speech during the annual summit of the Halifax International Security Forum, Politico reported Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Washington Post editorial warns of potential Ukraine abandonment under Trump
item.description: The Washington Post’s editorial board released an op-ed on Saturday detailing the potential threats to Ukraine and the U.S. if President-elect Trump moves to strike a deal with his Russian counterparts. “The reason for this surge in fighting is clear: Mr.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: As fast fashion's waste pollutes Africa's environment, designers in Ghana are finding a solution
item.description: ACCRA, Ghana (AP) — In a sprawling secondhand clothing market in Ghana’s capital, early morning shoppers jostle as they search through piles of garments, eager to pluck a bargain or a designer find from the stalls selling used and low-quality apparel imported from the West.
item.dc:creator: FRANCIS KOKUTSE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former New York resident arrested over plan to support ISIS
item.description: An Albanian man with dual citizenship in the U.S. was charged Friday for attempting to provide material support to the Islamic State and distributing instructional material on making explosives, according to the Department of Justice (DOJ).
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Philippine vice president makes public assassination threat against country's president
item.description: Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte said she has contracted a hired killer to assassinate her country's president in a bizarre public threat made amid worsening political divisions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fighting between armed sectarian groups in restive northwestern Pakistan kills at least 37 people
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Fighting between armed Sunni and Shiite groups in northwestern Pakistan killed at least 37 people and injured 25 others, a senior police officer from the region said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A look inside the most expensive house in the world — the home of the UK’s monarch
item.description: Buckingham Palace in London is worth about $4.9 billion and has about 775 rooms, including royal and guest rooms, offices and bathrooms.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Northern Haiti airports reopened to US flights
item.description: U.S. flights to six airports in northern Haiti can resume this week, according to the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA). The restart comes after the FAA prohibited U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Ukrainians less hopeful about future: Gallup
item.description: Ukrainians today are less optimistic for the future, nearly three years since Russia invaded the country, according to a new poll. The recent Gallup survey asked Ukrainian residents on a scale of one to 10 — where 10 is the best life — to rate their lives today. The result was an average of 6.9.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, The Hill

item.title: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers make last-ditch effort to safeguard US support
item.description: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers in both parties are working to get key provisions surrounding U.S. support for Kyiv passed into law by January, an effort to safeguard assistance that is under threat in a transition to a Trump administration and full GOP control of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Israel keeping its ‘eyes open’ for Iranian attacks during Trump transition period, ambassador says
item.description: Israel's Ambassador to the United Nations Danny Danon speaks to Fox News Digital about the threat from Iran during the Trump transition period.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Catherine Herridge wants Trump 'sit-down' on bipartisan press bill
item.description: “This case involves the First Amendment and the right of reporters in the future,” Catherine Herridge said.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu ‘in very bad company’ with ICC indictment: William Cohen
item.description: The ICC issued arrest warrants for Netanyahu and a former defense minister on war crimes allegations.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: US citizen among 4 dead in Laos after suspected alcohol poisoning
item.description: Four people have died after drinking tainted alcohol in Laos after officials said that multiple people had been sickened in town popular with backpackers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia launched intercontinental ballistic missile toward Ukraine, air force says
item.description: Russian troops launched an intercontinental ballistic missile from the Astrakhan region in an early morning attack on Ukraine on Thursday, according to Ukraine's Air Force.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Archaeologists discover 12,000-year-old pebbles that could provide new insights about the wheel
item.description: Researchers gave a detailed look into perforated stones collected out of excavations in Israel, and came to the conclusion that they could have served as spindle whorls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chief opponent to Uganda's president appears in court days after going missing
item.description: Kizza Besigye, a prominent critic of Ugandan President Yoweri Museveni, appeared in a military court Wednesday after being reported missing in Kenya.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine fires several US-made longer-range missiles into Russia for the first time
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine fired several American-supplied longer-range missiles into Russia, officials said Tuesday, marking the first time Kyiv used the weapons that way in 1,000 days of war.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9
